steps.title: Identify what carpa theater's stylistic influence means
steps.content: The historian describes this influence as “audience-mediated slapstick comedy.” This means two things: exaggerated physical humor (slapstick) and performances shaped by the audience's active reactions.

steps.title: Find the choice showing slapstick and audience participation
steps.content: "The company presented actos, short comedy sketches, that often relied on exaggerated physical humor to groups of agricultural workers, whose reactions—enthusiastic cheers of appreciation and, occasionally, loud boos of disapproval—promoted improvisation." includes both exaggerated physical humor and audience reactions shaping the performance, so D is correct.
